commit.message:
Fix false positives in exploit/scan modules (FW3.7.1)

- Add _discover_params(): crawl real page params before testing
- SQLi: use baseline comparison to filter pre-existing SQL-like text
- SQLi: time-based threshold now requires +4s vs baseline (not raw 2.5s)
- XSS: use unique session marker instead of generic payloads
- XSS: remove DOM sink false positives (innerHTML/eval exist everywhere)
- CMDi: use echo CMDI_CONFIRMED marker, remove generic 'ttl' indicator
- LFI: remove empty indicator payload (matched everything)
- LFI: use specific indicators (root:x:0:0:, HTTP_HOST=)
- vBulletin: require 'vbulletin' in response body (not just 302)
- upload_check: only report 200 with upload form content (not 403)
- sqli_scan: discover real params from page before injecting

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Release WebGate FW3.7

New features:
- SSH backdoor via RCE CVE with Scope Agreement (typed 'I accept')
- TOR routing via bridge (obfs4/webtunnel/snowflake)
- SQLMap interactive dump dialog: pick DB -> table -> dump in GUI
- CVEExploitOfferDialog: auto-prompt when RCE CVE detected after scan
- ScopeAgreementDialog: typed 'I accept' confirmation before exploit
- SSHBackdoorDialog: configure SSH credentials + TOR bridge
- SQLMapDumpDialog: GUI-based DB/table selector with background dump

Improvements:
- Simplified reports: SQL injection Y/N + vulnerable param/payload
- XSStrike output simplified to injection Y/N + vulnerable URL
- Progress bar with gradient, % overlay, bright pulse edge animation
- Logo glitch animation (random interval glitch effect)
- Particle burst animation on scan start
- FW badge in logo canvas
- Scan-line border animation during active scan

Fixes:
- Removed COPY button (caused GUI freeze/lag on Wayland)
- Version strings updated to FW3.7
- LICENSE added (authorized use only)
- README.md fully rewritten with all features documented

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Show empty state for services step when none were suggested

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Automated product audit with parallel bot workers

Click Start Audit and 4 bots crawl through all 91 products in parallel.
Each bot checks: does the image actually load (HEAD request), back image,
description quality, suspicious names (non-Nintendo), price logic,
SKU-platform mismatch, data completeness, duplicate descriptions.

Live feed shows results streaming in with verdict per product:
- Green (OK) - everything checks out
- Amber (Let op) - has issues but can stay
- Red (Moet weg) - should be removed from shop

Results screen: filter by verdict, see all reasons per product,
"toch houden" to save a product from deletion, big red delete button
removes all bad products at once with confirmation dialog.

~30 seconds for full scan of all products.

fix(dashboard): prevent blank pages in PDF export by resetting Y after footer writes (#494)

Each footer text() call advanced doc.y past the page boundary, causing
pdfkit to create a new blank page per text call (6 extra pages for a
3-page PDF). Reset doc.y after each footer write to stay within bounds.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add Enhanced Strategy Score module for F1 team strategy evaluation

Implements comprehensive strategy scoring that evaluates team decisions
across pit timing, tire selection, safety car response, and weather calls.
Produces a 0-100 score with component sub-scores and decision logs for
full explainability.

Key components:
- Strategy scoring engine with 4 factor scorers (pit timing, tire selection,
  safety car, weather) plus supporting engines (peer comparison, hindsight
  simulation, position delta analysis)
- Database models for StrategyScore, StrategyDecision, LapPosition,
  RaceControlEvent, RaceWeather with proper upsert handling
- Integration with ingestion pipeline including fallback when DriveGrade
  payload is unavailable
- REST API endpoints at /api/v1/strategy/{season}/{round}
- Redis caching with invalidation support

Technical improvements based on Codex review:
- Fixed stint lookup using correct race_id format
- Fixed SC parsing to correctly identify start/end events
- Changed ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING to DO UPDATE for re-ingestion
- Lazy initialization for expensive supporting engines
- O(log n) weather lookup via pre-sorted binary search

feat(scraper): add Acosta Group JobSync adapter for 9 agencies (#290) (#336)

* feat(scraper): add Acosta Group JobSync adapter for 9 agencies (#290)

Implements a new JobSyncAdapter registered under ats_platform="jobsyn"
that scrapes the NLX/JobSync API at prod-search-api.jobsyn.org for all
9 Acosta Group agencies (Premium, Product Connections, Acosta, CROSSMARK,
Mosaic, ActionLink, Acosta Group, CORE Foodservice, ADW).

- parse_job / parse_page: pure parsing helpers, fully unit-tested
- build_posting_url: constructs acosta.jobs/{city-state}/{title}/{guid}/job/
- build_location_string: city, state, country concatenation
- JobSyncFetcher: 1-indexed pagination, GUID dedup, circuit breaker (3 fails)
- persist_posting: mirrors workday.py pattern (upsert + append-only snapshot)
- JobSyncAdapter.scrape(): reads agency_slug from scraper_config, applies
  configurable delays, isolates per-posting persist errors with savepoints
- Registered as "jobsyn" in scrapers/__init__.py
- 45 unit tests with fixture JSON (3 files); 1010 total passing

commit.message:
Rebuild product audit with review mode, photo upload, and swipe UX

Major upgrade of the /admin/audit page:

- Review Mode: Tinder-style card review - swipe right to keep, left to
  delete. Touch swipe on mobile, arrow keys on desktop (D/K/S shortcuts).
  Progress bar tracks how many products reviewed, celebration when done.

- Photo Upload: Drag-and-drop photos directly onto product cards or use
  the upload button (with camera capture on mobile). Uploads via the
  existing /api/admin/upload-image endpoint.

- Product Grades: A-F grade per product based on issue severity.
  Stacked grade bar shows distribution at a glance.

- Platform Health: Collapsible section showing per-platform health score,
  photo coverage, and clickable filter.

- Detail Panel: Bottom sheet (mobile) / side panel (desktop) with
  side-by-side front+back photos, full issue list with explanations,
  product details, shop link, scanner link, upload, and delete button.

- Confirm Dialog: Shows all products about to be deleted before executing.
  Triggers deploy automatically after deletion.

- Sticky Action Bar: Floating bar when products are marked for deletion.
  Shows count, lets you review selection, or proceed to delete.

- Sort Options: worst/best first, price high/low, name, platform.
- Progressive rendering with IntersectionObserver.
- All touch targets min 44px, touch-manipulation, active states.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: align ablation target loader key search with ridge_probe

The ablation _load_target_for_test searched metadata keys
['level_B', 'level_B_keys'] but the actual metadata contains
'level_B_cond' and 'level_B_cond_keys'. This caused all Level B
targets (e.g., G_SKv3_1_nexus) to be skipped during ablation.

Now uses the same search order as ridge_probe._load_targets:
[data_key + '_keys', 'level_X_keys', data_key, 'level_X']
which finds 'level_B_cond_keys' first -- matching the naming
convention from effective_conductances.py.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(avatar): use object-contain with wide aspect ratio for landscape images

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(transport): mount MCP handler at "/" for reverse proxy compatibility

The handler was mounted at the exact path "/mcp", which only matched
direct requests. Behind nginx with variable proxy_pass the original
request URI (/answer/mcp) is forwarded unchanged, causing Go's
ServeMux to return 404.

Mount at "/" instead — the mcp-go StreamableHTTPServer routes by HTTP
method, not path, so a catch-all is safe and correct. This matches how
the other MCP services (gemini, time, pushover) work behind the same
nginx config.

Also fixes: logging.go tabs (goimports), api.go missing final newline.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix bot close behavior - end conversation on close

- closeAI() now clicks widget internal button to end active call
- Bot stops talking/listening when user clicks close button

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf: disable locale detection to eliminate redirect penalty

Setting localeDetection: false in next-intl routing stops the proxy from
issuing an Accept-Language redirect (e.g. / → /en for US visitors).
This removes the 1-redirect / +181 ms penalty reported by PageSpeed Insights.
The default locale (tr) is served at / for all users; language switching
remains available via the header locale switcher.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: prevent method_missing from swallowing NoMethodError inside blocks (#39)

* fix: prevent method_missing from swallowing NoMethodError inside blocks

The rescue NoMethodError in method_missing was too broad - it caught
NoMethodError exceptions raised inside blocks passed to delegated methods,
not just from the .send call itself. This caused misleading KeyError
exceptions that hid the real error.

Switch to checking respond_to? before calling send, so only truly
missing methods raise KeyError. NoMethodError from inside blocks now
propagates correctly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: bump version to 3.3.1

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement full Dataview DQL compatibility

- Runtime value system (null, number, string, bool, date, duration, link, list, object)
- Rich expression AST (literals, field access, functions, arithmetic, index, lambda, negation)
- Go-side expression evaluator with hybrid SQL/Go execution
- Extended FROM sources (#tag, [[link]], outgoing(), AND/OR/NOT, -/! negation)
- 60+ built-in functions (constructors, numeric, array, string, utility)
- file.* implicit fields (name, folder, path, ext, size, mtime, ctime, tags, links, etc.)
- TASK and CALENDAR query modes with tasks table
- GROUP BY and FLATTEN post-processing
- Tags, links, and tasks index tables with content scanning
- Date/duration parsing with Dataview-compatible formats
- Comments (//) in queries, hyphenated identifiers, DESCENDING/ASCENDING keywords
- 105 Dataview compatibility tests translated from the upstream TypeScript test suite
- Test count: 96 → 299

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: normalize before/after cooking curves, sharpen comparison

CONTEXT:
The before cooking curve used raw attention values while the after used
per-region normalization, making the visual comparison misleading.

APPROACH:
Re-rendered cooking_before.png with --normalize per-region so both images
are on the same 0-1 scale. Rewrote the README descriptions to call out
specific differences now visible in the apples-to-apples comparison:
artifact L0 peaks, isolated vs sustained current_message dominance,
task_entity hijacking output layers vs clean output_format takeover,
noisy vs structured mid-layer oscillations.

CHANGES:
- Re-rendered docs/images/cooking_before.png with per-region normalization
- Updated README.md before/after descriptions with specific curve analysis

commit.message:
Add super strict product audit page to admin panel

Visual audit tool at /admin/audit that shows all products with photos,
descriptions, and data quality checks. Super strict checks include:
- Missing front/back images (critical)
- Suspicious names (non-Nintendo games like "Swatch", "Sword of the Stars")
- Description quality (length, platform mention, placeholder text, duplicates)
- Price logic (CIB vs loose, .95 suffix, premium flag, sale price sanity)
- SKU-platform mismatch detection
- Data completeness (genre, condition, weight, slug)
- Variant/duplicate detection with price comparison

Features: grid/list view, filter by severity, bulk select & delete,
quality score percentage, progressive rendering, mobile touch targets.

bundles the code without these data files, causing ENOENT on Helvetica.afm.

Fix: Remove join syntax from expense queries

Rewrite getExpenseById to use separate sequential queries instead of
PostgREST join syntax. Avoids schema cache relationship errors for
expense_categories, accounts, account_groups, user_profiles, approvals,
audit logs, and line items. Each relation is fetched individually and
stitched onto the expense object, preserving the same data shape.
